title: Module 1
content:
Overview
of
Analytical
equipment:
Beer-Lambert's
Law-
Colorimeters - Spectrophotometry (UV/ Visible, IR): Instrumentation -
Filters - Monochromators - Detectors - Automated clinical Biochemistry
analysers – Applications. Electroanalytical Instruments: Methods -
Ion selective electrode (ISE) - Blood Gas Analysers - Electrolyte
Analysers - Blood cell counters. Sample preparation equipment -
centrifuge, sonicator

title: Module 2
content:
Analytical techniques in Biochemistry and Molecular biology:
Radioimmunoassay (RIA) - Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent Assay
(ELISA/CLIA) - Applications; Polymerase Chain Reaction- RT PCR;
Principle of NMR spectroscopy and Mass spectrometry. Principle and
applications
of
Electrophoresis
(Gel,
Capillary
and
IEF),
Chromatography (Gas and HPLC), Flow Cytometry.
(Class Assignment/Seminar topics: Lab on a Chip (LOC) biochemical
sensor, Miniaturized Systems for (Bio) Chemical analysis and synthesis
- glucometer-Point of Care Test equipment (POCT))

title: Module 3
content:
Bioelectric Devices: Electrocardiograph- Pre-amplifiers- Filters-
Isolation circuits- RL driven circuit- protection circuits; Analog front
end of ECG machine (Block diagrams only). Patient monitoring
systems
–
Instrumentation
of
Pulse
oximeter,
Arrhythmia
monitors,Ambulatory
recorders
-
Holter
monitors.
Electroencephalograph - Instrumentation, Types of Evoked potentials,
Apnoea monitors. BP measurement - Working principle of manual
sphygmomanometer

title: Module 4
content:
Cardiac output measurement- Fick method -Dilution Methods-
Doppler
method, Impedance Cardiography. Spirometry – Basic system and
applications-Pulmonary function measurements: Respiratory volumes,
lung
capacity
-different
volume
measurement.
Impedance
Plethysmography. Blood flow meters - Electromagnetic – Ultrasonic
Doppler blood flow meters. Audiometers – Pure tone - speech
audiometers.
